recommendation.reasoning: The decision to not pursue the SearchBoost Query Engine is driven by significant risks related to authentication weaknesses and data handling compliance, which are critical for maintaining user trust and legal adherence [risk-001, risk-004]. Additionally, the product exhibits a concerning drop in search relevance, particularly for anonymous users, leading to increased support tickets and potential user dissatisfaction, which further compounds the feasibility issues [customer-001, customer-002]. The combination of these high-severity risks and the failure to mitigate critical issues necessitates this recommendation.

executive_summary:
The SearchBoost Query Engine is a proposed product designed to enhance search relevance and performance across various user segments, particularly focusing on anonymous users. This product aims to address critical issues identified in the analysis, including a significant drop in search relevance for anonymous users [customer-001] and a concerning increase in support ticket volume due to irrelevant search results [customer-002]. The opportunity lies in improving user satisfaction and engagement through more accurate and relevant search outcomes, which is essential for maintaining competitive advantage in the market.

However, the analysis has revealed several substantial risks and challenges that hinder the viability of the SearchBoost Query Engine. Key blockers include identified authentication weaknesses [risk-001] and data handling compliance risks [risk-004], both of which pose significant legal and operational threats. Additionally, three unmitigated high risks exceed the acceptable threshold, leading to a failed risk gate. The analysis also highlighted a critical instrumentation gap for new query types [metrics-004] and a notable drop in the search relevance score, which is the North Star metric for this product [metrics-001]. These findings indicate that while there are opportunities for improvement, the associated risks and potential negative impacts on user experience are considerable.

Given the high level of risk and the critical nature of the identified issues, the recommendation is to not pursue the SearchBoost Query Engine at this time, with a confidence level of 85%. Immediate next steps for the team should include conducting a thorough review of the identified risks, particularly focusing on authentication and data compliance, and exploring alternative solutions that can address the search relevance challenges without incurring the same level of risk. Additionally, a legal review is necessary to ensure compliance with PII and privacy regulations before any further action is taken [risk-004].

key_findings.title: Significant Drop in Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
key_findings.description: The new ranking algorithm introduced in v2.4.1 has led to a 15% drop in search relevance scores, particularly affecting anonymous users who make up approximately 40% of queries. This segment was not adequately represented in pre-launch A/B testing, resulting in a significant degradation of search results for navigational queries.
recommendations: Re-run A/B test scoped to anonymous users with properly powered sample size.
Patch ranking model for anonymous user fallback path.
assumptions: Anonymous users rely on the search engine without prior session data.

key_findings.title: Search Relevance Score Drop
key_findings.description: The recent deployment (v2.4.1) has caused a significant drop in search relevance scores, which could lead to decreased user satisfaction and increased support volume. This is a critical issue that needs immediate attention.
recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Consider a rollback if no quick fix is found.
assumptions: The drop in relevance is directly related to the new ranking algorithm introduced in v2.4.1.

key_findings.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
key_findings.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric reflects the quality of search results and is critical for user satisfaction and engagement.
recommendations: Monitor search relevance scores closely and investigate factors contributing to the drop.
assumptions: Search relevance directly correlates with user satisfaction and engagement.

key_findings.title: Instrumentation Gap for New Query Types
key_findings.description: There is a significant gap in instrumentation for new query types such as image search and voice search. Without analytics events for these types, we lack visibility into their performance and user engagement.
recommendations: Add baseline instrumentation for image search and voice search to enable future experimentation.
assumptions: Instrumentation is necessary to measure the success of new features.

key_findings.title: Remediate Search Relevance for Anonymous Users
key_findings.description: Address the 15% drop in search relevance scores for anonymous users caused by the new ranking algorithm in v2.4.1.
recommendations: Conduct a root-cause analysis of the ranking algorithm.
Implement changes to improve relevance for anonymous users.
assumptions: The new ranking algorithm can be adjusted to improve relevance for anonymous users.

key_findings.title: Increased Support Ticket Volume Due to Irrelevant Results
key_findings.description: Following the v2.4.1 deployment, support ticket volume increased threefold, primarily due to users experiencing irrelevant search results. This indicates a significant user dissatisfaction and potential churn risk.
recommendations: Implement a remediation plan to improve search relevance.
Monitor support ticket trends closely to gauge user sentiment.
assumptions: Users expect search results to be relevant and aligned with their queries.

key_findings.title: Mobile Search Latency Increased Significantly
key_findings.description: Post-deployment, mobile search latency increased to 890ms, significantly impacting user experience. This latency issue is likely contributing to the negative feedback from mobile users.
recommendations: Profile ranking re-ranker for mobile session path to identify optimization opportunities.
assumptions: Users expect fast response times from search functionalities.

key_findings.title: Feature Flag Dependency for Rollback
key_findings.description: The ability to rollback the v2.4.1 deployment is blocked by a dependent feature flag change. This needs to be addressed to allow for quick remediation of the relevance score drop.
recommendations: Identify and resolve the feature flag dependency.
assumptions: The feature flag change is the only barrier to rollback.

key_findings.title: Increased Support Volume
key_findings.description: The spike in support volume due to irrelevant search results indicates a significant user experience issue. This could lead to further dissatisfaction and churn if not addressed promptly.
recommendations: Prioritize fixing the ranking algorithm to improve relevance.
Monitor support tickets closely for trends.
assumptions: The increase in support volume is directly correlated with the changes made in v2.4.1.

key_findings.title: Search Latency Issue on Mobile
key_findings.description: The increased search latency on mobile web indicates a potential performance issue with the new ranking model. This needs to be profiled and optimized to prevent SLO breaches.
recommendations: Profile the new ranking model and optimize for mobile.
assumptions: The new ranking model is causing the increased latency.

north_star.title: North Star Metric: Search Relevance Score
north_star.description: The North Star metric for SearchBoost is the Search Relevance Score, which measures the effectiveness of search results as perceived by users. This metric reflects the quality of search results and is critical for user satisfaction and engagement.

input_metrics.title: Primary KPI: Search Click-Through Rate (CTR)
input_metrics.description: The Search Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of users who click on search results after performing a search. This is a primary KPI that indicates user engagement with search results.

guardrails.title: Guardrail Metric: Session Abandonment Rate After Search
guardrails.description: The Session Abandonment Rate After Search measures the percentage of sessions where users abandon the session after performing a search. This guardrail metric must not degrade as it indicates user dissatisfaction.
